threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the 56 bus because you think it’ll take forever—locals know it’s the quickest way to get to the heart of Dalston.
Last night on the 47, a couple argued over the best kebab spot in the area. Turns out, the best ones are still tucked away behind the new flats.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I watched my neighbor argue passionately with the kebab vendor over the best spices. It felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's normal to see a street musician serenading a crowd while a spontaneous dance-off breaks out. Pure Berlin chaos and creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by their unflinching tolerance for the smell of burnt bread from Tartine—it's like perfume to them.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing like watching a Muni bus halt for a street performer mid-song, only to see commuters nodding along like it’s part of the routine.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it’s all about the Sagrada Família, but the real charm is in those hidden plazas where locals sip coffee far from the tourist buzz.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they stare at the metro map like it’s a treasure map, despite the clear signs everywhere.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think ‘the Lido’ means just one place, not the half-dozen spots that stir arguments on sunny days.
Caught a corner of the 59 bus last night; a chap was serenading a sleeping lady with opera. Only in Brixton would this be a Tuesday night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I stepped over a random pile of discarded furniture and saw a family of raccoons moving in like they owned the place.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice Berliners will carefully place their trash to balance on top of each other instead of just using the bin? It's a whole art form.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their love for that hidden taco truck on 24th—it’s not on Google Maps, just a tip from a true Missionite.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus with a saxophonist belting out Coltrane. Totally out of place, yet perfectly San Francisco. Only here, folks!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think they can just grab a coffee at any plaza, but only locals know which cafés serve the real deal. It’s a taste test!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newbie by their bewildered look when the metro stops unexpectedly. That Sagrada Família to La Pau stretch can throw anyone off.
